args: Make tmux the single source of truth for the active window; the URL becomes a resumable bookmark used only on initial mount. Replace 2.5s SSE poll-as-primary with tmux hooks (after-select-window, after-new-window, after-kill-window, after-rename-window) installed once in ~/.rk/tmux.conf. Add 'rk notify' CLI subcommand that reads a per-socket lockfile written by 'rk serve' on startup, then POSTs to the running server to bump a snapshot generation counter; the SSE loop pushes a fresh snapshot in <100ms. Demote the 2.5s poll to a 10-15s safety-net backstop for missed hooks and unmanaged tmux servers. Frontend: delete the 3s userNavTimestamp debounce; on initial mount, fire one select-window to align tmux with the URL; afterwards derive the URL from currentSession.windows.find(w => w.isActiveWindow) via navigate({replace: true}) — URL is write-only. Sidebar clicks become pure mutations (POST .../select); UI never owns selection. Yanking other tabs on the same server to follow tmux is acceptable. Boards are orthogonal — they persist in @rk_board per-server tmux options keyed by stable window_id, and /board/$name routes ignore active-window changes. Free wins: instant board-cleanup when pinned windows die; optional 'tmux-active' badge on BoardPane. One refactor: move the prevIDs/currentIDs cleanup-diff out of the poll loop into the snapshot builder so it runs on every hook-driven snapshot. Open question: tmux servers not started via rk serve won't have ~/.rk/tmux.conf loaded — they fall back to the safety-net poll.
